title: Sibling to ISS-2373: renderFailureEntry "Dome's label:" prose-prefix implies dome convention but the value is our internal shorthand
description: In build-scripts/generate-html.js renderFailureEntry, each silent-failure card displays a line reading "Dome's label: REMOVED 2026-03-15" (or SUSPENDED 2026-03-15). The dome_label field in uncounted-failures.json is OUR internal tracking shorthand (we observed the entry was removed on 2026-03-15 via commit diff; the date is OUR observation, the verb is OUR shorthand). The prose-prefix "Dome's label:" attributes this string to the dome as if the dome publishes these strings as labels. The dome does NOT label entries this way — the prior bracket-strawman ISS-2373 covers the [REMOVED]/[SUSPENDED] case but does not address the prose-prefix attribution. Fix: rename the prefix to "Effective status:" or "Our tracking label:" (and consider whether the dome_label value should be normalized to e.g. "silently removed 2026-03-15" — but that may be scope creep since the existing values are short and the prefix change alone resolves the attribution problem). Defender impact: minor (the bracket strawman is the worse problem; the prefix is a softer attribution that a careful defender could still cite, but it is one click less obvious). Routing: please assign to analyst-baby or analyst, depending on cycle availability — the work is small (one find-replace in generate-html.js).

proposed_patch.rationale: Renames the prose-prefix from "Dome's label:" (attributes string to dome) to "Effective status (our tracking label):" (makes our attribution explicit). Same template-literal in renderFailureEntry; uniquely-occurring in generate-html.js (verify with grep before apply).
